title: 1.1: States that the Scapy runtime reports version 2.5.0.dev87 via both scapy.__version__ and conf.version to demonstrate programmatic access methods for version checking in scripts.

1.2: Displays the full startup banner when the fancy banner is enabled, including the ASCII-art logo, “Welcome to Scapy”, version identifier 2.5.0.dev87, GitHub URL, "Have fun!" line, and the random quote block.
1.4: States that after importing scapy.all, conf.layers is a scapy.config.LayersList containing 1319 entries.

1.5: States that the 1319 figure refers to layer classes registered in conf.layers at runtime, not a count of protocol-layer source files in the Scapy repository which distinguishes loaded functionality from code structure.
1.6: States that the default verbosity level conf.verb at startup is 2 to establish the baseline diagnostic output behavior.

1.7: States that conf.verb = 0 is silent, conf.verb = 1 is minimal output, conf.verb = 2 is normal chatty output, and higher values (3 or more) are increasingly verbose to enable appropriate debugging output control.

1.8: States that on the described Linux/WSL2 system, conf.L3socket, conf.L2socket, and conf.L2listen are PF_PACKET-based sockets implemented in scapy.arch.linux for layer‑3, layer‑2, and layer‑2 listening respectively which identifies the platform-specific packet capture mechanism.
1.9: States that IP() / ICMP() creates a packet whose top-level object is an IP packet containing an ICMP payload followed by a NoPayload terminator to clarify the packet construction model.

1.10: States that each header (IP, ICMP) is its own Packet subclass instance, linked through .payload and .underlayer, and that specific layers can be accessed with pkt[IP] and pkt[ICMP] which explains the object-oriented architecture for packet manipulation.

1.11: States that when Scapy is used as a non-interactive library conf.color_theme is NoTheme from scapy.themes, in the interactive console conf.interactive is True and conf.color_theme is DefaultTheme() from scapy.themes to show context-dependent display formatting.
2.1: Report that the execution was clean without the CryptographyDeprecationWarning regarding TripleDES/Blowfish that appear during the Scapy import.

2.2: Reports a list of multiple possible "random quotes" (e.g., citing distinct examples from the source code like Sebastien Chabal AND Lao-Tze) instead of the single, specific quote that appeared in the startup banner during the actual investigation
